title: Marsdenia acuta
intro: Marsdenia acuta, also known as the "Kadukkai" or "Vasa" in traditional medicine, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia acuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into herbal formulations such as powder, decoction, and oil, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Marsdenia acuta is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The Apocynaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that are found in various parts of the world. The plants within this family are often characterized by their trumpet-shaped flowers and milky sap. Marsdenia acuta, in particular, is a species of flowering plant that is native to Australia.

It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its potential use in treating certain health conditions.
